items.title: Two projects, conflicting versions
items.scenario: One app needs openai 0.28 (old API) and another needs openai 1.x (new client).
items.explanation: Each project gets its own venv, so both versions coexist on the same machine without conflict.

items.title: Reproducible teammate setup
items.scenario: A new teammate clones the repo and must run it with the same dependencies.
items.explanation: They create a venv and pip install from requirements, getting an environment matching yours.

items.title: Clean CI builds
items.scenario: A CI job should start from a known-clean dependency state every run.
items.explanation: The pipeline creates a fresh venv per build so leftover packages can't mask missing requirements.

items.title: Keeping system Python clean
items.scenario: You want to try a heavy library without permanently changing your machine.
items.explanation: Install it inside a venv; deleting the .venv folder fully removes it.
